title: Compact card density for token consumers
goal: Add a compact card-density/profile option that de-duplicates repeated non-claim boilerplate while preserving mandatory negative semantics at the artifact or section level.

acceptance.assertion: A compact profile reduces repeated per-card non-claim text without removing the non-claims from the consumable artifact.
Existing verbose/default card outputs remain compatible or migration is explicit.
A before/after token or byte-size comparison is recorded for representative bundles.

metadata.source_gap: Max-dump critique: repeated does_not_establish lines are token-expensive for agents.

workspace.initial_test_failure: The isolated read-only role exposed /usr/bin/python3 without pytest; the writer patch and review remained valid and were preserved.
workspace.recovery: The workspace was collected and closed with exact hashes and no discarded changes. The test harness was converted to standard-library unittest in the preserved task worktree and revalidated through normal Grabowski execution.

validated_acceptance.rpu-v1-t018-density: produce_lens_card_collection(density=compact) removes only repeated per-card does_not_establish arrays and retains the complete mandatory tuple once at collection level.
validated_acceptance.rpu-v1-t018-compat: The established produce_lens_card and produce_lens_cards implementations and default outputs are unchanged; verbose collection mode retains self-contained cards.
validated_acceptance.rpu-v1-t018-token-proof: The committed proof records deterministic JSON sizes for three representative cards: 1799 verbose bytes, 1274 compact bytes, 525 bytes saved, 29.2 percent reduction.

validation.post_merge_ci: all observed required checks passed on the merged implementation

evidence.summary: All post-merge workflows passed, including test-suite, CodeQL, Lens Model, lint, contracts and task-index.
